food_subgroup: Tofu and soy-based dishes with rice and vegetables

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a composed tofu rice bowl with vegetables and vinaigrette dressing. FOODON_00002809 (tofu-based food product) is the closest defensible parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers tofu as an ingredient. No composite bowl code exists; closest parent used for the primary protein component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 102263004 covers tofu as a substance. No composite dish code exists for a tofu rice bowl; closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 400 g, representing a full bowl comprising rice, scrambled tofu, roasted potatoes, corn, tomatoes, lettuce, spring onions, and dressing as served at Khao – Funan, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated composite from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Tofu (raw, firm), White rice (cooked), Potato (baked), Corn (cooked), Tomato (raw), Lettuce (raw), Spring onion (raw); soy vinaigrette estimated from soy sauce and vinegar components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service – FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists for this composite restaurant dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component assembly: approximately 35% cooked jasmine rice, 25% firm tofu (scrambled), 15% roasted potato, 10% corn, 8% tomato and lettuce, 7% dressing/vinaigrette. USDA SR Legacy values used for each component. HPB Singapore Foodo does not carry this specific dish. Values represent a plausible central estimate for a 100 g portion of the assembled bowl.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 – Atkinson FS et al.; white rice GI ~72, potato GI ~78, composite bowl estimated at GI 56 weighted by carbohydrate contribution of rice, potato, corn, and tofu components
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite dish. GI estimated by carbohydrate-weighted average of primary glycemic contributors: cooked white rice (GI 72, ~35% of bowl weight, ~28 g carb per 100 g bowl), roasted potato (GI 78, ~15% of bowl weight, ~13 g carb), corn (GI 52, ~10% of bowl weight, ~8 g carb), tofu and vegetables (GI ~15, ~40% of bowl weight, ~7 g carb). Weighted GI = (28×72 + 13×78 + 8×52 + 7×15) / 56 ≈ 56. GL per 400 g serving = (56 × 74) / 100 = 41.4. Source: Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021;114(5):1625-1632.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from soy sauce vinaigrette and house dressing
hypertension.guidance: At approximately 210 mg sodium per 100 g (840 mg per 400 g serving), this bowl contributes roughly 37% of the 2,300 mg daily sodium limit recommended by the American Heart Association; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-soy-sauce dressing or consume a smaller portion to manage sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index driven by white rice and roasted potato content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated GI of 56 (medium) and a glycemic load of approximately 41 per full serving, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider requesting a smaller rice portion or substituting brown rice to reduce postprandial glucose excursion, while the tofu and vegetable components provide beneficial protein and fibre to moderate glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat, plant-based protein, and vegetable-rich composition
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The bowl's low saturated fat content (~0.4 g per 100 g), absence of cholesterol, and plant-based protein from tofu (which contains isoflavones associated with modest LDL reduction) make it a generally heart-favourable choice, though sodium from the soy vinaigrette should be monitored in individuals with established cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Tofu Everyday Bowl is a contemporary healthy-eating dish served at Khao – Funan in Singapore. It features scrambled firm tofu as the primary protein, served alongside fresh tomatoes and crisp lettuce, layered over roasted potatoes and sweet corn kernels, with sliced spring onions, all presented on a bed of fragrant jasmine rice tossed in a light soy vinaigrette. The dish is finished with the restaurant's house-made signature dressing. The scrambled tofu provides a soft, slightly chewy texture reminiscent of scrambled eggs, while the roasted potatoes add a crisp exterior and fluffy interior. The soy vinaigrette imparts a savoury, mildly tangy flavour profile. Nutritionally, the bowl is a balanced plant-based meal providing approximately 118 kcal per 100 g, with 5.2 g protein, 2.8 g fat, and 18.5 g carbohydrate. It is naturally cholesterol-free and low in saturated fat, making it suitable for cardiovascular health. The medium glycemic index (~56) and moderate glycemic load (~41 per full serving) mean individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should moderate portion size, particularly the rice and potato components. Sodium content (~210 mg per 100 g) from the soy-based dressing warrants attention for those managing hypertension. The dish is fully vegan, halal-friendly, and dairy-free, aligning with multiple dietary preferences common in Singapore's multicultural food landscape. It is positioned as a weekday health-conscious meal option within the bento and grain-bowl category.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists for this composite restaurant dish in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central). Values are estimated by weighted component assembly using USDA SR Legacy data for individual ingredients. GI is estimated by carbohydrate-weighted average from University of Sydney GI Database reference values for component foods. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated from USDA SR Legacy; no exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN for this specific dish. Values should be validated against laboratory analysis or restaurant-provided nutrition data if available. 2. Sodium estimate of 210 mg per 100 g is based on a moderate soy vinaigrette application; actual sodium may vary significantly depending on dressing quantity used by the restaurant. 3. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as this is a fully plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients; confirmed zero by composition — plant foods do not naturally contain vitamin B12. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 as no vitamin-D-fortified ingredients are present and plant foods are not a natural source of vitamin D; confirmed zero by composition. 5. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as this is a fully plant-based dish; naturally zero by composition. 6. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as no hydrogenated fats are used; confirmed zero by composition. 7. GI of 56 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this dish exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 8. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data. 9. Sentinel value -1 is not used in this record; all fields are either measured/estimated numeric values or confirmed zeros with justification above.
